skill.name: add-event-type
skill.description: Add a new event type to the frontend feed system with corresponding React component. Use when user mentions "new event", "add event type", "event block", "new block type", or wants to display new agent output types.
skill.summary: Guide adding typed frontend feed events and React blocks.
skill.author: AppleLamps

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged external commands and weak cryptography, but review found Markdown code formatting and TypeScript examples only. No executable scripts, shell commands, network calls, credential access, cryptography, or prompt injection were found in SKILL.md. The skill is safe to publish.

low_findings.title: Static External Command Findings Dismissed
low_findings.description: The flagged locations are Markdown inline code references and TypeScript example blocks. They do not execute shell, Ruby, or external commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines contain Markdown backticks and static TypeScript snippets only. No command invocation syntax, script file, or runtime execution path is present.

low_findings.title: Static Weak Cryptography Finding Dismissed
low_findings.description: The flagged line is the skill description. No cryptographic function, hash algorithm, password handling, or encryption logic appears in SKILL.md.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 3 is plain frontmatter text describing frontend event handling. There is no semantic evidence of weak cryptography in the file.

content.value_statement: Frontend feeds need consistent event types and matching UI blocks. This skill guides Claude, Codex, or Claude Code through the required TypeScript and React updates.
actual_capabilities: Guides review of the EventType union in frontend/src/App.tsx.
Shows how to define a new typed event interface.
Directs adding the new type to the event union.
Guides creation of a React block component for the event.
Covers feed rendering and optional WebSocket handler updates.
Lists naming, styling, typing, and testing guardrails.
limitations: It is guidance only and does not include an automation script.
It assumes the feed logic lives in frontend/src/App.tsx.
It does not define backend payload generation.
It requires manual testing with a mock WebSocket event.

use_cases.title: Add Agent Output Blocks
use_cases.description: Add a new feed block for a new agent output category while keeping TypeScript coverage complete.

use_cases.title: Extend WebSocket Feeds
use_cases.description: Map a new backend message type into the frontend feed renderer and verify display behavior.

use_cases.title: Standardize Event UI Work
use_cases.description: Give contributors a repeatable checklist for adding event interfaces, components, and rendering cases.

prompt_templates.title: Add Simple Event
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to add a progress update event type to the frontend feed. Include the type, component, and renderer case.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for one new feed event.

prompt_templates.title: Create Error Block
prompt_templates.prompt: Add an error event block that displays a title, message, and severity using existing feed component styles.
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate UI extension with structured fields.

prompt_templates.title: Connect Backend Event
prompt_templates.prompt: Add a search result event type and update the WebSocket handling path if the backend sends this event.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced frontend and message handler update.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Event Consistency
prompt_templates.prompt: Review the feed event implementation and identify every change needed for a new task summary event.

output_examples.input: Add a search result event type.
output_examples.output: The assistant identifies the event fields, adds the typed interface, creates a matching feed block, and wires the renderer case.

output_examples.input: Create a block for displaying errors.
output_examples.output: The assistant designs an error event shape, follows existing visual patterns, and keeps the feed rendering type-safe.

output_examples.input: Add progress updates from WebSocket messages.
output_examples.output: The assistant updates the frontend event model, checks message handling, and recommends a mock WebSocket test.

best_practices: Review existing block components before adding a new one.
Use complete TypeScript fields instead of loose values.
Test rendering with a realistic mock WebSocket event.
anti_patterns: Do not add an event type without updating the renderer.
Do not use any for new event payload fields.
Do not create styling that conflicts with existing feed blocks.

faq.question: What does this skill help build?
faq.answer: It helps add a new frontend feed event type and a matching React block component.

faq.question: Does it generate code automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides implementation steps that an AI coding assistant can follow.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: It is marked for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Does it require backend changes?
faq.answer: Only if the backend must send the new event type through WebSocket messages.

faq.question: What files does it mention?
faq.answer: It specifically mentions frontend/src/App.tsx as the expected feed implementation file.

faq.question: How should changes be tested?
faq.answer: Test with a mock event sent through the WebSocket path or equivalent feed input.
